tasks.subject: [merge-conflict-prevention] [P1] Add timestamp id generator .claude/scripts/id.ts
tasks.description: Create .claude/scripts/id.ts exporting: generateId(now?: Date): string producing a UTC YYMMDD-HHMMSS id (e.g. 260703-081234) via an injectable clock (default new Date()); isTimestampId(s): boolean and isLegacyId(s): boolean (4-digit zero-padded like 0294); parseIdDate(id): Date|null. Zero runtime deps; runs under node --experimental-strip-types. Add __tests__/id.test.ts (Vitest): format is exactly YYMMDD-HHMMSS, zero-padded; injected clock is respected; two ids from clocks one second apart differ and sort chronologically as plain strings; isLegacyId matches 0001..0299 and isTimestampId matches 26xxxx forms; a legacy id sorts lexicographically before any timestamp id.

tasks.subject: [merge-conflict-prevention] [P1] Ephemeral-repo merge harness proving prevention (with negative controls)
tasks.description: Add a Vitest suite that, per case, does mkdtemp -> git init -> configure user -> base commit -> two divergent branches -> git merge with the DEFAULT driver -> assert. Cases: (A) NEGATIVE CONTROL old scheme: array manifest with nextNumber where both branches unshift a new entry and both bump nextNumber -> assert the merge CONFLICTS (exit nonzero / conflict markers). (B) NEW scheme: object-keyed manifest where each branch adds a different timestamp-id key -> assert CLEAN auto-merge, valid JSON, both keys present, no markers. (C) NEGATIVE CONTROL changelog union: multi-section [Unreleased] with merge=union where both branches append to different sections -> assert entries interleave/mis-file OR duplicate (document the observed failure). (D) NEW changelog: per-section sentinel anchors where each branch inserts above the same section anchor -> assert CLEAN auto-merge with the default driver, both entries present in the correct section, no markers. Real git in temp dirs (no mocking); clean up dirs in afterEach. Each test carries a purpose comment. This is the validation gate the whole spec rests on: it must be able to fail.

tasks.subject: [merge-conflict-prevention] [P2] Reshape decisions/ and specs/ manifests to id-keyed (version 2) via a migration script
tasks.description: Write .claude/scripts/migrate-manifests-to-id-keyed.ts that reads the current {version, nextNumber, [array]} manifests and emits {version: 2, decisions|specs: { <idKey>: {id, ...entry} }} keyed by id. Legacy numeric entries keep their zero-padded number as the string key (e.g. '0310') and gain an id field equal to that key; drop the top-level nextNumber. Preserve every existing field verbatim (slug, title, status, created, extractedFrom, specSlug, project). Run it once to convert both decisions/manifest.json and specs/manifest.json in place, and keep the script for reference. Validate both files still JSON.parse and contain the same entry count as before.

tasks.subject: [merge-conflict-prevention] [P2] Update spec-manifest-ops.ts + adr-drift-check.mjs for id-keyed manifests + timestamp allocation
tasks.description: Update .claude/scripts/spec-manifest-ops.ts: change readManifest/writeManifest and every command (add, update-status, get, list, audit, fix, remove, archive) to operate on the id-keyed object shape (version 2); the add command allocates a timestamp id via id.ts instead of reading/incrementing nextNumber, and writes filename/dir using the id. Keep the CLI surface stable. Update .claude/scripts/adr-drift-check.mjs to read the id-keyed decisions manifest, accept both legacy numeric and timestamp id filenames (0NNN-slug.md and <id>-slug.md), and flag duplicate ids. Run adr-drift-check after the migration and confirm zero false drift. Update any unit tests for these scripts.

tasks.subject: [merge-conflict-prevention] [P2] Point ADR/spec templates + adr commands + guides at timestamp ids; add .gitattributes
tasks.description: Update the host ADR/spec authoring surfaces to timestamp ids + id-keyed manifest: .claude/commands/adr/create.md and .claude/commands/adr/from-spec.md (stop reading nextNumber; allocate an id via id.ts / spec-manifest-ops; filename <id>-slug.md; add an id-keyed manifest entry), and the writing-adrs and managing-specs skills. Add a root .gitattributes documenting the optional local mergiraf driver for decisions/manifest.json + specs/manifest.json (commented/opt-in) and marking pnpm-lock.yaml so it is not blindly line-merged. Do NOT enable merge=union on the changelog (superseded by the sentinel approach in task 3.1).
tasks.activeForm: Updating templates, adr commands, and gitattributes

tasks.subject: [merge-conflict-prevention] [P3] Per-section CHANGELOG sentinel anchors + populator insert-above + dedup
tasks.description: Add a fixed anchor comment '<!-- FLOW:ADD-ABOVE <Section> -->' at the bottom of each [Unreleased] subsection (### Added, ### Changed, ### Removed) in CHANGELOG.md. Update .claude/git-hooks/changelog-populator.py so each new bullet is inserted immediately ABOVE the matching section's anchor (creating the section + anchor if absent) rather than appended, and add a dedup pass that removes exact-duplicate bullets within a section. Preserve existing behavior for released sections. Confirm the hook still runs (a test commit populates above the anchor) and does not disturb prior entries.
tasks.activeForm: Adding changelog sentinels and updating the populator

tasks.subject: [merge-conflict-prevention] [P4] Flow plugin: delegate id-allocation to host tooling (PR to dork-labs/marketplace)
tasks.description: In the marketplace repo (/Users/doriancollier/Keep/dork-os/marketplace, plugin dir plugins/flow), edit skills/specifying-work/SKILL.md steps 7-8 to stop describing 'numbered from decisions/manifest.json nextNumber, increment it' and instead delegate id-allocation + manifest maintenance to the host's tooling (mirror the pattern executing-specs already uses: 'use your harness's manifest-maintenance command/script; otherwise edit the entry directly; skip if no manifest'). Edit templates/docs/adr.md: number: NNNN -> id:, drop the increment comment, filename pattern <id>-slug.md. Keep the plugin harness-neutral (no dorkos-specific timestamp scheme). Run the plugin's own tests (npm test / vitest) green. Open a PR on a new branch, coordinated with the in-flight flow-plugin-extraction work (DOR-133); do not merge. Follow the marketplace repo's CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md.
tasks.activeForm: Delegating id-allocation in the flow plugin

tasks.subject: [merge-conflict-prevention] [P5] Document the id convention, changelog sentinel, and optional driver
tasks.description: Write a contributing/ guide covering: the timestamp-id convention for new ADRs/specs (YYMMDD-HHMMSS, legacy frozen, sort behavior), the id-keyed manifest shape, the CHANGELOG sentinel-anchor workflow, and the optional local mergiraf setup. Update the AGENTS.md Artifacts section to state the new id convention for new ADRs/specs. Reflect id allocation in writing-adrs/managing-specs if not already covered by task 2.3. Keep prose free of em-dashes.
